title: Total sales for one region
prompt: Using the sales table below, write a formula in F2 that adds up the Amount (column C) for every row where the Region (column B) is "North".

dataset.note: Headers in row 1; data spans rows 2 to 6.

explanation: SUMIFS(sum_range, criteria_range1, criteria1) adds only the values whose row meets every criterion. Here the three North rows (1200 + 1500 + 600) total 3300. Note that in SUMIFS the sum range comes first, unlike SUMIF.
hint: In SUMIFS the range to add comes first, then each criteria range and its matching criterion.

title: Two conditions at once
prompt: Write a formula in F2 that sums the Amount (column C) only for rows where the Region (column B) is "North" AND the Rep (column A) is "Amit".

dataset.note: Headers in row 1; data spans rows 2 to 6.

explanation: SUMIFS supports multiple criteria pairs, all of which must be TRUE for a row to count. Amit's North rows are 1200 and 600, totalling 1800. The order of the criteria pairs does not change the result.
hint: Add a second criteria_range / criteria pair after the first one; both must match.

title: Predict the SUMIFS result
prompt: Given the table below, what does =SUMIFS(C2:C6, B2:B6, "South") return?

dataset.note: Headers in row 1; data spans rows 2 to 6.

explanation: Three rows have Region "South": Priya (900), Rahul (1500) and Amit (600). Their Amounts sum to 3000.
hint: Find every row where Region equals South, then add only those Amounts.

title: Fix the swapped arguments
prompt: This formula is meant to total the Amount (column C) for the "North" region, but it returns a #VALUE! error because the arguments are in the wrong order. Fix it.

dataset.note: Headers in row 1; data spans rows 2 to 5.

explanation: In SUMIFS the order is sum_range, then criteria_range, then criteria. The broken formula put the criterion text in the criteria_range slot. The correct version sums column C where column B equals "North": 1200 + 1500 = 2700.
hint: Remember the SUMIFS order: the numbers to add come first, then the range to test, then the value to test for.

title: Greater-than criterion
prompt: An expenses sheet has Amount in column B (B2:B7). Which formula totals only the amounts greater than 1000?
explanation: Comparison operators in criteria must be written as text inside double quotes, like ">1000". The sum_range and criteria_range can be the same column when you are filtering on the values you are also summing.
hint: A comparison criterion has to be a quoted string, and SUMIFS still needs sum_range first.

title: Cell reference in criteria
prompt: A dashboard lets the user type a region name into cell E1. You want F1 to total the Amount (C2:C6) for whatever region is in E1, updating automatically. Which formula is correct?
explanation: To make a criterion dynamic, reference the cell directly (E1) without quotes. Quoting it as "E1" would look for the literal text E1, and "=E1" is invalid syntax inside a criteria argument.
hint: A bare cell reference picks up whatever the user types; quotes would turn it into literal text.
